title: Pick up/rewrite #910: async-boundary callstack capture (javascript.options.asyncstack)
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

Stefan (2026-06-20): revive #910 (Ayushsunny, 2021) so researchers can analyze callstacks ACROSS ASYNC BOUNDARIES (setTimeout / promises / fetch chains), not just the synchronous stack.

#910's actual content is minimal: set Firefox pref enabling async-stack capture + a setTimeout test page. REWRITE NEEDS:
1. FIX the typo: #910 sets 'javascript.option.asyncstack' (singular) — the real pref is 'javascript.options.asyncstack' (plural). As written it's a DEAD pref (the new scripts/verify_obsolete_prefs.py harness would flag it INVALID). Verify the corrected pref is LIVE in FF152.
2. Integrate with the RESTORED callstack instrument (#1178, FF152-fixed stackDump) — confirm that with the pref ON, the captured callstacks actually include async-parent frames (the JS engine maintains the async stack chain; verify the privileged stackDump path sees it). This is the real validation, not just flipping the pref.
3. Add it to the prefs dict (interlocks with feat/firefox-prefs-dict), modernize the test page (Set_Timeout.html) into a proper test asserting async frames are captured in the callstacks table.
3. Gate it appropriately (a browser_param flag, since async stacks add overhead). RELATES-TO #1178 (callstack instrument).
